questions.question: How do I replace a lost ID?
questions.normalized_question: how do i replace a lost id
questions.answer:
**How to replace a lost ID**
Office involved: **Class Adviser; Admin Office; School Cashier; School Head**

**Steps:**
1) Report the loss to your **Class Adviser** immediately.
2) Get and fill out the **ID Replacement Request Form**.
3) Class Adviser endorses to **Admin** for record verification.
4) Request is forwarded to the **School Head** for approval.
5) After approval, **Admin** or **Class Adviser** issues a new ID or temporary gate pass.

Reference: **DepEd Order No. 41, s. 2012; School-based policy under School Head authority.**

questions.question: How do I replace a lost report card?
questions.normalized_question: how do i replace a lost report card
questions.answer:
**How to replace a lost report card**
Office involved: **Class Adviser; Registrar; School Head**

**Steps:**
1) Submit a **written request** to your **Class Adviser**.
2) Adviser evaluates and endorses to the **Registrar**.
3) Registrar verifies your academic records.
4) Request goes to the **School Head** for approval.
5) Registrar releases a **certified true copy** of the report card.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 8, s. 2015; DepEd Order No. 41, s. 2012.

questions.question: How do I enroll?
questions.answer:
**How to enroll**
**Office involved:** Enrollment Committee; Registrar; Guidance Office

**Steps:**
1) Get an **enrollment form** from the **Registrar**.
2) Submit the accomplished form plus requirements (**SF9**, **Birth Certificate**, **JHS Certificate of Completion**, etc.).
3) Registrar evaluates and validates the records.
4) Guidance Office confirms strand placement if applicable.
5) You are officially enrolled after confirmation and encoding.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 3, s. 2018; Annual DepEd Enrollment Guidelines Memorandum.

questions.question: What are the enrollment requirements?
questions.answer:
**Enrollment requirements**
**Office involved:** Registrar

**Steps:**
1) Prepare required documents.
2) Submit documents for evaluation.
3) Compliance is confirmed before enrollment.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 58, s. 2017; DepEd Order No. 3, s. 2018.

questions.question: Where can I get the enrollment form?
questions.normalized_question: where can i get the enrollment form
questions.answer:
**Where to get the enrollment form**
**Office involved:** Registrar

**Steps:**
1) Inquire at the **Registrar’s Office**.
2) Enrollment form is issued and explained.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 3, s. 2018.

questions.question: How do I request school forms?
questions.normalized_question: how do i request school forms
questions.answer:
**How to request school forms**
You can request documents such as:
- **Good Moral Certificate**
- **Certificate of Enrollment**
- **SF9 / Report Card**
- **SF10**
- **F137**

Please tell me which **document** you need.

questions.question: How do I request a Good Moral Certificate?
questions.normalized_question: how do i request a good moral certificate
questions.answer:
✅ **Good Moral Certificate**
**Office involved:** Registrar; School Head if approval is needed

**Steps:**
1) Secure and accomplish the request form or use the online form.
2) Submit the request to the **Registrar**.
3) Registrar verifies your records and purpose.
4) For cases needing approval, it is endorsed to the **School Head**.
5) Release is done by the **Registrar**.

⏱ **Processing time:** **3–7 working days**
💰 **Fee:** **FREE**

📌 Request here:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdJ8jZXFnllT0lpGBtW4onQZTi0aUKzr6TI2nQ3N00sQd8r1w/viewform

questions.question: How do I request a Certificate of Enrollment?
questions.normalized_question: how do i request a certificate of enrollment
questions.answer:
✅ **Certificate of Enrollment**
**Office involved:** Registrar; School Head if approval is needed

⏱ **Processing time:** **3–7 working days**
💰 **Fee:** **FREE**

📌 Request here:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdJ8jZXFnllT0lpGBtW4onQZTi0aUKzr6TI2nQ3N00sQd8r1w/viewform

questions.question: How do I request SF9?
questions.answer:
✅ **SF9 / Report Card**
**Office involved:** Class Adviser; Registrar; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Submit a written request or request through the **Registrar**.
2) Registrar checks and verifies academic records.
3) If needed, the request is endorsed for approval.
4) A certified copy or official record is prepared.
5) Release is done by the **Registrar**.

⏱ **Processing time:** **3–7 working days**
💰 **Fee:** **FREE**

questions.question: How do I request a report card?
questions.normalized_question: how do i request a report card
questions.answer:
✅ **Report Card / SF9**
**Office involved:** Class Adviser; Registrar; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Submit a written request to your adviser or registrar.
2) The school verifies your academic record.
3) Approval is secured when required.
4) A certified true copy or official record is prepared.
5) Release is done by the **Registrar**.

⏱ **Processing time:** **3–7 working days**
💰 **Fee:** **FREE**

questions.question: How do I request SF10?
questions.answer:
✅ **SF10**
**Office involved:** Registrar; School Head if approval is needed

⏱ **Processing time:** **3–7 working days**
💰 **Fee:** **FREE**

📌 Request here:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdJ8jZXFnllT0lpGBtW4onQZTi0aUKzr6TI2nQ3N00sQd8r1w/viewform

questions.question: How do I request F137?
questions.answer:
✅ **F137**
**Office involved:** Registrar; School Head if approval is needed

⏱ **Processing time:** **3–7 working days**
💰 **Fee:** **FREE**

📌 Request here:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdJ8jZXFnllT0lpGBtW4onQZTi0aUKzr6TI2nQ3N00sQd8r1w/viewform

questions.question: When will requested documents be released?
questions.normalized_question: when will requested documents be released
questions.answer:
**When will requested records or documents be released?**
**Office involved:** Registrar

**Steps:**
1) Request is logged and acknowledged by the **Registrar**.
2) Records are verified and prepared.
3) Approved documents are released within the processing period.

**Standard processing time:** **3–7 working days**

**Reference:** DepEd Citizen’s Charter; Republic Act No. 11032.

questions.question: Can someone else claim my documents on my behalf?
questions.normalized_question: can someone else claim my documents on my behalf
questions.answer:
**Can someone claim documents on your behalf?**
**Office involved:** Registrar

**Steps:**
1) Representative presents an **authorization letter**.
2) Submit **valid IDs** of both parties.
3) Registrar verifies records.
4) Documents are released to the authorized representative.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 41, s. 2012.

questions.question: Is there a fee for requesting certificates or school forms?
questions.normalized_question: is there a fee for requesting certificates or school forms
questions.answer:
**Is there a fee for requesting certificates or forms?**
**Office involved:** Registrar

**Process:**
1) Submit the request form to the **Registrar**.
2) Registrar verifies request and records.
3) Certificates or forms are processed **without payment**.
4) Document is released upon completion.

**Note:** Public schools do not collect fees for issuance of school records, certificates, or forms.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 46, s. 2008.

questions.question: How long does it take to process documents?
questions.normalized_question: how long does it take to process documents
questions.answer:
**How long does it take to process documents?**
**Office involved:** Registrar

**Steps:**
1) Request is officially recorded.
2) Documents are processed and verified.
3) Released within the prescribed timeline.

**Typical processing time:** **3–7 working days**

**Reference:** DepEd Citizen’s Charter; Republic Act No. 11032.

questions.question: What is the process for transferees or late enrollees?
questions.normalized_question: what is the process for transferees or late enrollees
questions.answer:
**Process for transferees or late enrollees**
**Office involved:** Registrar; Guidance Office; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Submit complete transfer credentials to the **Registrar**.
2) Registrar checks authenticity and completeness.
3) Guidance Office conducts assessment or interview.
4) School Head approves enrollment, subject to available slots.
5) Learner is officially enrolled upon approval.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 3, s. 2018; DepEd Order No. 54, s. 2016.

questions.question: How do I request a change of strand?
questions.normalized_question: how do i request a change of strand
questions.answer:
**How to request a change of strand**
**Office involved:** Guidance Office; Registrar; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Submit a written request to the **Guidance Office**.
2) Guidance Counselor assesses suitability.
3) Registrar reviews academic records.
4) School Head gives final approval.
5) Approved change is reflected in **Form 201**.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 51, s. 2014; DepEd Order No. 54, s. 2016.

questions.question: Can I switch strands in between semesters?
questions.normalized_question: can i switch strands in between semesters
questions.answer:
**Can I switch strands in between semesters?**
Yes, but you will have to take **irregular classes** for the subjects you may have missed.

questions.question: Can I transfer into or out of the school mid semester?
questions.normalized_question: can i transfer into or out of the school mid semester
questions.answer:
**Can I transfer into or out of the school mid semester?**
Yes, but you will have to take **irregular classes** for the subjects you may have missed.

questions.question: What happens if I have failing marks?
questions.normalized_question: what happens if i have failing marks
questions.answer:
**What happens if you have failing marks**
**Office involved:** Subject Teacher; Class Adviser; Guidance; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Subject teacher informs the learner about failing or at-risk performance.
2) Learner undergoes academic counseling and intervention.
3) Remedial or support programs are provided when applicable.
4) Performance is reassessed after intervention.
5) Final grades follow DepEd assessment guidelines.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 8, s. 2015; DepEd Order No. 73, s. 2012.

questions.question: What are the graduation requirements?
questions.answer:
**Graduation requirements**
**Office involved:** Subject Teachers; Class Adviser; Registrar; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Complete all required SHS subjects.
2) Secure academic clearances, forms, and required documents.
3) Registrar verifies records.
4) School Head confirms eligibility for graduation.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 36, s. 2016; DepEd Order No. 8, s. 2015.

questions.question: What is the process for dropping and adding subjects?
questions.normalized_question: what is the process for dropping and adding subjects
questions.answer:
**Process for dropping and adding subjects**
**Office involved:** Class Adviser; Registrar; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Submit a written request within the allowed adjustment period.
2) Class Adviser evaluates and endorses the request.
3) Registrar reviews subject load and records.
4) School Head approval is secured.
5) Records are officially updated.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 54, s. 2016.

questions.question: What is the process of dropping students?
questions.normalized_question: what is the process of dropping students
questions.answer:
**Process of dropping students**
**Office involved:** Class Adviser; Guidance; School Head; Registrar

**Steps:**
1) Learner is identified due to absences, academic deficiency, or disciplinary concerns.
2) Counseling and parent or guardian conference is conducted.
3) Due process and documentation are completed.
4) School Head issues decision.
5) Registrar records the dropping officially.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 92, s. 2012; DepEd Order No. 8, s. 2015.

questions.question: What is the absence policy?
questions.answer:
**Excuse letter or absence policy**
**Office involved:** Class Adviser

**Steps:**
1) Submit a written excuse letter signed by parent or guardian upon return.
2) Attach supporting documents if applicable.
3) Class Adviser validates the excuse.
4) Attendance records are updated.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 8, s. 2015.

questions.question: How many absences are allowed before failing or dropping?
questions.normalized_question: how many absences are allowed before failing or dropping
questions.answer:
**How many absences are allowed before failing or dropping?**
**Office involved:** Class Adviser; Guidance Office

**Steps:**
1) Attendance is monitored regularly.
2) Learners nearing the limit are notified.
3) Counseling or intervention is provided.
4) Exceeding **20% of total school days** may result in dropping or failing, with due process.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 8, s. 2015; DepEd Order No. 3, s. 2018.

questions.question: How do I request the use of school facilities?
questions.normalized_question: how do i request the use of school facilities
questions.answer:
**How to request use of school facilities**
**Office involved:** Subject Teacher or Class Adviser; Property Custodian; School Head

**Steps:**
1) Submit a written request with purpose and schedule.
2) Adviser or subject teacher endorses the request.
3) Property Custodian checks availability and safety.
4) School Head gives final approval.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 40, s. 2015; DepEd Order No. 13, s. 2018.

questions.question: How is class standing computed?
questions.answer:
**How is class standing computed?**
**Office involved:** Subject Teacher

**Steps:**
1) Written works are assessed.
2) Performance tasks are evaluated using rubrics.
3) Quarterly assessments are administered.
4) Final grades are computed following DepEd policy.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 8, s. 2015.

questions.question: What is the school contact information?
questions.normalized_question: what is the school contact information
questions.answer:
**Contact Information**
Office involved: **School Administration; Registrar**

You may contact the school through the official channels below:

Phone: **(046) 872-0411**
Email: **342602@deped.gov.ph**
Facebook Page: **https://web.facebook.com/DepEdTayoSHSwithinBES342602?_rdc=1&_rdr#**
Address: **Tincoco St., Campo Santo, Bacoor City, Cavite**

For **enrollment**, **documents**, and **student concerns**, please contact the **Registrar** or **School Administration** during office hours.

You may also ask about:
- school address
- how do I enroll
- enrollment requirements

questions.question: Can you tell me about the school?
questions.normalized_question: can you tell me about the school
questions.answer:
**About the School**
You may ask about:
- **school address**
- **who can apply**
- **academic strands offered**
- **class size**
- **learning modes**
- **scholarships**
- **eligibility requirements**

questions.question: What is the school address?
questions.answer:
**School Address**
Tincoco St. Campo Santo Brgy. Poblacion, City of Bacoor, Cavite 4102.

questions.question: Who can apply?
questions.answer:
**Who can apply**
All incoming **Grade 11** and **Grade 12 transferee** Filipino or foreign students for **S.Y. 2025–2026** are welcome to apply.

questions.question: What academic strands are offered?
questions.answer:
**Academic Strands Offered**
We offer academic strands such as **STEM** and **HUMSS**.

For more detailed information, please visit the **Academic Information** page.

questions.question: What is the class size?
questions.answer:
**Class Size**
Both **Grade 11** and **Grade 12** have **30 to 40 students per section**.

questions.question: What learning modes are available?
questions.answer:
**Learning Modes**
We offer flexible learning modes to suit students’ capacity and internet access.

Students can choose:
- **Remote online learning** and join regular synchronous classes
- **Remote offline learning** and pick up digital modules from the school at regular intervals

questions.question: How much is the uniform?
questions.answer:
**How much is the uniform?**
**Office involved:** School Administration; Authorized School Supplier

**Process:**
1) School announces the approved uniform design and price at the start of the school year.
2) Learners inquire from the school or authorized supplier.
3) Uniforms are bought from the authorized supplier.

**Note:**
-> Uniform is encouraged but must not be used as basis to exclude learners from classes.
-> Prices depend on the student’s size.
-> For more information, please proceed to the **school admin**.

**Reference:** DepEd Order No. 46, s. 2008; DepEd Order No. 88, s. 2010.

questions.question: Are learning modules or books available?
questions.normalized_question: are learning modules or books available
questions.answer:
**Learning Modules / Books**
Books are available and can be accessed at the **LRMDS**.

questions.question: Are scholarships available?
questions.answer:
**Scholarships**
No, we do not provide scholarships.

questions.question: What are the eligibility requirements?
questions.answer:
**Eligibility Requirements**
The **HUMSS** strand does not have eligibility requirements.

The **STEM** strand requires at least a **general weighted average of 85 in all subjects**.
